title: MathLesson Training Pack 03 — Export Layout Lab
description: A focused training lesson for Print/PDF controls, Reveal slide breaks, visual block placement, and export preflight workflow.

sections.label: Start
sections.title: What this lesson tests
sections.content:
Export Layout Lab

This lesson teaches the layout controls that help Print/PDF and Reveal exports behave predictably.

What this lesson tests
This file tests page breaks, slide breaks, keep-together wrappers, allow-break wrappers, graph page classes, landscape print sections, wide tables, and pre-export review behavior.
Author task
Import this lesson, run the Reveal Export Planner, run Print/PDF checks, then export a student lesson and verify that the content remains readable.

sections.label: Breaks
sections.title: Page and slide breaks
sections.content:
Page and Slide Breaks

Print/PDF page breaks and Reveal slide breaks are separate controls.

Print/PDF page break
Use <hr class="page-break"> where a printed/PDF page should start cleanly.

This paragraph starts after an explicit Print/PDF page break.

Reveal slide break
Use <hr class="slide-break"> or <!-- slide --> where a Reveal slide should split.

This paragraph starts after an explicit Reveal slide break.

Alternative Reveal split marker:
<!-- slide -->

sections.label: Keep
sections.title: Keep-together and allow-break wrappers
sections.content:
Keep-Together and Allow-Break Wrappers

Keep together
This block is wrapped in avoid-break. The theorem statement and formula should stay together when possible.
$$\int_a^b f'(x)\,dx=f(b)-f(a)$$

This ordinary prose block is wrapped in allow-break. It may split if needed. Use this for long explanations that do not need to remain visually intact. Do not use it for diagrams, graphs, proofs, or tightly coupled equations unless you are comfortable with a split.

UX observation prompt
Note whether these controls are easy to insert from the current UI. If not, this becomes a redesign requirement.

sections.label: Visuals
sections.title: Graphs and visual blocks on their own page
sections.content:
Graphs and Visual Blocks

Large visuals usually need their own page or slide.

Visual export decision
flowchart LR
  A["Visual block"] --> B["Own slide"]
  A --> C["Own page"]
  B --> D["Less crowding"]
  C --> D

Use the export planners to decide whether this visual should remain with nearby explanation or be isolated.

sections.label: Wide
sections.title: Wide table and landscape print section
sections.content:
Wide Table and Landscape Section

Wide tables are a common source of ugly PDF output. This example is intentionally wide.

Block typeAuthor concernPrint concernReveal concernStudent concernRecommended action
MermaidSyntax safetyKeep togetherOwn slideRendered diagramUse quoted labels
JSXGraphBoard sizeStatic fallback riskOwn slideInteractive boardCheck student export
PlotlySlider rangesGraph pageOwn slideResponsive plotUse preflight checks
TableWidthLandscape sectionMay overflowScroll riskSplit columns

sections.label: Preflight
sections.title: Pre-export workflow
sections.content:
Pre-Export Workflow

  1. Import or open the lesson.
  2. Render every section once.
  3. Run Prototype Audit.
  4. Run Reveal Export Planner if slides are needed.
  5. Run Print/PDF preview if printed output is needed.
  6. Add only the layout controls the planner or preview actually justifies.
  7. Export student lesson and test quizzes, exercises, and major visuals.
Author task
After testing this file, write down the first three actions that were hard to find in the current UI.

quiz.stem: Which marker starts a new Print/PDF page?
quiz.feedback: The page-break class is for Print/PDF page control.

quiz.stem: Which marker is intended for Reveal slide splitting?
quiz.feedback: The slide-break class is used for Reveal slide splitting.

quiz.stem: Every graph-heavy section should be checked with export planners before final delivery.
quiz.feedback: True. Graph-heavy sections are common overflow risks.

quiz.stem: Record one layout action that should be easier to find in the redesigned UI.
quiz.feedback: This response feeds directly into the UX/UI redesign requirements.
